title: Profiling, Allocator Performance, and Hardening · Memory Trace: Hardening Options
summary: A low-latency gateway reduces allocations but increases peak memory, fragmentation, and tail pauses under changed traffic. Planned artifact: Add and verify the cmem hardening options increment in measured allocator optimization report. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Evaluate allocator checks, guard pages, canaries, randomization, control-flow defenses, fortification, and production cost.
Compare system allocator, mimalloc, jemalloc, arenas, and pools using representative workloads and operational constraints.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Hardening Options · Theory Hardening Options
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory native-systems team handles memory case 9103. Produce bounded C evidence to compare live, reserved, and peak memory before optimizing; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates.

steps.instruction:

It must compare live, reserved, and peak memory before optimizing, reject one changed boundary, print an observable result and invariant, and return nonzero when the invariant fails. Keep instant code within the PicoC 3.2.2 practice subset; do not claim C23, optimizing compiler, sanitizer, ABI, thread, operating-system, FFI, load, or production evidence until its explicit controlled transfer gate passes.
